name: Pat Nixon's White House Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together sugar and butter in an electric mixer until fluffy.
2. Add vanilla and lemon rind, blending well.
3. Add eggs one at a time, mixing thoroughly after each addition.
4. Remove the mixture from the mixer.
5. Gradually add cake flour, stirring with a heavy mixing spoon to combine.
6. Chill the dough in the refrigerator for 1 hour or overnight.
7. Roll the dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch and cut out shapes using cookie cutters.
8. Bake at a preheated oven temperature of 375 degrees Fahrenheit until brown, about 7-8 minutes.
Note: If colored granulated sugar is used for decoration, prepare an egg wash by mixing one well-beaten egg with 1 tablespoon milk. Brush the egg wash over the cut-out dough and sprinkle with sugar before baking. This recipe makes approximately 10 dozen cookies.
